description_long: This bollard features an ornate, decorative design with a robust, flared base and intricate sculptural details resembling stylized scrolls and floral motifs. The body is solid and appears to be made of cast metal, with a dark gray finish and subtle highlights that accentuate its elaborate carvings. The upper section is bulbous and adorned with leaf-like patterns, while the base is wide and curved, providing stability and a visually imposing presence. The surface is hard and slightly matte, with a texture that suggests durability and resistance to weathering. There are no visible openings, chains, or connectors, and the object is clearly intended as a fixed barrier rather than a movable or interactive element. Its overall appearance is reminiscent of classical or Victorian urban street furniture, suitable for use in upscale public spaces or historical districts.
description: A highly decorative, cast metal bollard with ornate scrollwork and a flared, curved base, finished in dark gray. The design is robust and classical, with intricate surface details and a solid, immovable presence.
description_view_0: Front view shows the symmetrical scrollwork and the full width of the flared, decorative base.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the depth of the sculpted scrolls and the curvature of the base, with more shadowing on the right.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the central bulbous section and the layered, leaf-like patterns above the scrolls.
description_view_3: This side reveals the rounded contours of the base and the intricate, raised details on the lower section.
attribute_cap_design: bulbous, decorative finial with leaf motifs
